findings: Static validation matches the approved T14 scope: the implementation remains limited to per-symbol exit-rule handling and does not expand into batch execution or final reporting logic.
Stop-loss and take-profit rules still evaluate against current bar.close with inclusive thresholds, satisfying the price-trigger contract.
Raw intents now pass through unchanged when no exit trigger fires, preserving same-step sell and buy behavior in the non-trigger path.
When an exit trigger fires, same-step intents are replaced by exactly one deterministic full-position sell generated from the current open position quantity.
Flat-position raw sell behavior remains preserved because exit-rule evaluation only runs when a position is already open.

Holding-bar timing matches the approved planner behavior: new positions are created with holding_bar_count = 0, exit rules evaluate against the start-of-step count, and increment occurs only after fills on a surviving position with a bar present.
The max-holding regression evidence is consistent with the required behavior: the close occurs on 2024-01-04, not earlier, for the 2024-01-01 / 2024-01-03 / 2024-01-04 bar sequence.
Diagnostics on the modified engine and exit-rule test files are clean.
summary: The final implementation satisfies the T14 acceptance criteria and the holding-bar timing fix aligns with the approved planner anchors.
